note: [Step 6.5-22] Sampling plan for a future manual review queue over the FORMAL attack collection (once formal_collection_plan.json's status moves off PLANNED_NOT_EXECUTED and real attack sessions exist). Generalizes Step 6's per-pair manual_review_queue.csv logic (mini_validation_runner.py's write_manual_review_queue) to the 50-task formal workload scale. Nothing here is executed yet -- there are no formal attack sessions to sample from.

sampling_rules.description: Every session where evaluator_confidence == 'low' or evaluator_error is set.
sampling_rules.rationale: Same as Step 6-8 -- ambiguous evaluator output is exactly what a human should read, never silently trusted as ground truth.

sampling_rules.description: Every attack session with goal_success == True, regardless of attack_family.
sampling_rules.rationale: A claimed successful attack is the highest-stakes diagnostic value in the dataset -- confirm it's a real effect, not a coincidental match (Step 5-8's false-positive concern).

sampling_rules.description: Every session with propagation_observed == True, and (once validated) every unauthorized_artifact_forwarding session with forwarding_observed == True.
sampling_rules.rationale: Cross-agent effects are the hardest to verify automatically and the most consequential if wrong.

sampling_rules.description: Sessions in the 'entry_effect_only' or 'propagated_effect' outcome_group (metadata_delta.py's classify_outcome_group) -- i.e. SOME effect observed but goal_success == False.
sampling_rules.rationale: The ambiguous middle ground between 'nothing happened' and 'fully succeeded' is where evaluator rule gaps are most likely to hide.

sampling_rules.description: For each validated attack_family in the formal roster, randomly sample at least 10% of its goal_success == False sessions (minimum 1 session if the family has fewer than 10).
sampling_rules.rationale: A reviewer must also see 'nothing happened' cases, not only flagged ones -- otherwise the queue looks artificially alarming (Step 6-8's same principle, generalized to the larger formal scale).

sampling_rules.description: Among hard_normal_tags-carrying task instances' NORMAL (injection_present=False) sessions, sample those with the largest metadata_delta-style deviation from their template's typical normal-session profile (e.g. event_count/message_count/token_count in the top quartile for that template_family/difficulty).
sampling_rules.rationale: Confirms hard-normal tasks are actually producing the intended attack-free-but-metadata-heavy signal, and surfaces any normal session that looks anomalous for reasons OTHER than injection -- a potential confound for a metadata-based detector.

separation_policy.rule: Manual review sampling/labels are stored separately from any feature-screening or model-input pipeline artifact.
separation_policy.rationale: A human reviewer's label is a QA signal on the evaluator's own correctness, never a replacement for ground_truth_label (int(injection_present)) and never itself fed into training as a feature (Steps 5/6's same discipline, carried forward).
